methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A chip and semiconductor company founded by James and Oliver Proud, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $100M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip companies, with particular relevance to 3 same-stage private peers: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money, yields $2.0B. Peer-median valuation across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median of $2.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across the three public semiconductors) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data, standard for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B, with a median of $2.0B. Both applicable methods converge on the $2.0B midpoint. Key uncertainties include wide dispersion within the peer set ($500M to $5.0B), Substrate's specific technical differentiation relative to Etched and d-Matrix, and the absence of revenue or unit economics to anchor relative value. Stage timing and capital efficiency remain material but are not quantified in available data.
